engineGuide.h2: Nissan Hypermini EV Component Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every motor, battery and electronics code fitted to the Nissan Hypermini across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK EV specialists. Select any component code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 20kW Electric Motor
entries.history: The EM57-type AC synchronous motor powered all Hypermini variants, delivering 27hp with instant torque and regenerative braking capability for urban microcar efficiency.
compatibleVariants: Fleet Spec, Demo Spec, Export Spec, 2-Seater RHD, Museum Display
commonFailures: Motor bearing wear at 40k-70k miles (rotor imbalance, unusual whine)
Resolver position sensor failure causing limp mode or no-start
Thermal sensor degradation leading to overheating protection activation
entries.cta: Get quotes for EM57-type Nissan Hypermini electric motor replacement

entries.title: 288V Lithium-ion Battery Pack
entries.history: The LIP-XX lithium-ion pack represented Nissan's early high-energy density solution, offering superior range and cycle life for Fleet Spec Hypermini units.
compatibleVariants: Fleet Spec, Export Spec (late production), Collector Grade Road-legal
commonFailures: Individual cell voltage imbalance causing BMS fault codes
Thermal management pump failure leading to reduced capacity
Connector corrosion on high-voltage terminals (UK climate exposure)
entries.cta: Get quotes for LIP-XX Nissan Hypermini battery pack replacement

entries.title: 288V Lead-acid Battery Pack
entries.history: The LA-XX lead-acid pack powered early Demo Spec Hypermini units, offering lower cost but reduced range and cycle life versus lithium-ion.
compatibleVariants: Demo Spec, Early Fleet Spec (pre-production), Museum Static Display
commonFailures: Cell sulphation from infrequent use (common in collector vehicles)
Terminal corrosion causing high-resistance charging faults
Reduced capacity below 50% original after 8-10 years
entries.cta: Get quotes for LA-XX Nissan Hypermini battery pack replacement

entries.title: AC Motor Inverter Unit
entries.history: The INV-AC288 inverter converted DC battery power to 3-phase AC for the synchronous motor, with regenerative braking control integrated.
compatibleVariants: All variants (Fleet, Demo, Export, RHD, Museum)
commonFailures: DC-link capacitor degradation causing intermittent fault codes
IGBT module thermal cycling failure at high mileage
Cooling fan bearing wear leading to inverter overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for INV-AC288 Nissan Hypermini inverter replacement

entries.title: AC Onboard Charging Module
entries.history: The CHR-ONBD module enabled AC charging from JIS or Type 2 infrastructure, with communication protocol handling for early EVSE compatibility.
compatibleVariants: Export Spec, Late Fleet Spec, Road-legal Collector Grade
commonFailures: Charging port pin wear causing intermittent connection
Communication protocol mismatch with modern UK chargers
Internal relay failure preventing charge initiation
entries.cta: Get quotes for CHR-ONBD Nissan Hypermini charger replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Hypermini EV Component Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Hypermini's pioneering electric powertrain is generally robust, but battery cell degradation, motor bearing wear, and inverter capacitor failure dominate replacement enquiries for these ultra-rare 1999-2001 microcars. Every rebuilt component supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal parts designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: Battery Cell Degradation - Why Nissan Hypermini Range Drops Over Time
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Hypermini Fleet Spec, Demo Spec, Export Spec (1999-2001, LIP-XX & LA-XX battery packs)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 30k-60k miles or 8-12 years from first registration
problems.rootCause: Lithium-ion cells lose capacity through cycle ageing and calendar degradation; lead-acid cells suffer sulphation from infrequent use.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: BMS recalibration, individual cell testing and balancing, software update
repairOptions.longevity: Restores 10-20% capacity; suitable for early degradation under 70% SOH

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed cells within pack, reseal housing, full capacity test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores 80-90% original capacity; viable if pack housing intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit refurbished LIP-XX/LA-XX pack with upgraded cells, BMS calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Full range restoration; recommended for packs below 50% SOH or with multiple failed cells

problems.recommendation: For Hypermini collectors, if cell balancing restores range to acceptable levels, it's the most cost-effective solution. However, if multiple cells have failed or the pack is over 10 years old, a refurbished pack with warranty offers better long-term value and preserves vehicle provenance.
problems.cta: Compare refurbished LIP-XX battery prices for your Nissan Hypermini

problems.h4: Motor Bearing Wear - Why Nissan Hypermini EM57 Motors Develop Whine
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Hypermini all variants (1999-2001, EM57-type AC synchronous motor)
problems.rootCause: Rotor bearings experience high radial loads from instant torque delivery; lubrication breakdown and contamination accelerate wear in urban stop-start use.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Motor removal, bearing inspection, re-lubrication, reseal, dyno test
repairOptions.longevity: May extend life 10k-20k miles; only viable if bearings show minimal wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt motor with upgraded ceramic bearings, resolver calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Full performance restoration; recommended for bearings with audible noise or play

problems.recommendation: If bearing noise is intermittent and mileage is under 50k, inspection and lubrication may suffice. However, once audible whine or vibration is present, bearing replacement requires full motor disassembly - at that point, a rebuilt unit with upgraded bearings and warranty is the smarter investment for rare EV preservation.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt EM57-type motor for your Nissan Hypermini

problems.h4: Inverter Capacitor Failure - Why Nissan Hypermini Power Electronics Fault
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Hypermini all variants (1999-2001, INV-AC288 inverter unit)
problems.rootCause: DC-link electrolytic capacitors degrade with thermal cycling and age; capacitance loss causes voltage ripple, triggering fault codes or intermittent power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Inverter removal, capacitor replacement with high-temp units, reseal, bench test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability for 20k-40k miles; viable if IGBTs and control board intact

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt INV-AC288 with upgraded capacitors, IGBT testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Full electronics restoration; recommended for units with multiple fault codes or physical damage

problems.recommendation: For Hypermini owners experiencing intermittent inverter faults, capacitor replacement is often cost-effective if caught early. However, if the unit has logged multiple fault codes or shows physical corrosion, a rebuilt inverter with warranty provides greater peace of mind for these ultra-rare components.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt INV-AC288 inverter prices for your Nissan Hypermini

problems.h4: Charging Port Incompatibility - Why Nissan Hypermini Won't Charge on Modern UK Infrastructure
problems.affectedModels: Nissan Hypermini Export Spec, Late Fleet Spec (2000-2001, CHR-ONBD charger module)
problems.rootCause: Original JIS charging protocol and physical connector differ from modern UK Type 2/CCS infrastructure; communication handshake failures prevent charge initiation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit JIS-to-Type 2 adapter cable with protocol converter, safety certification
repairOptions.longevity: Enables charging on most UK public chargers; does not modify vehicle electronics

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CHR-ONBD with Type 2-compatible unit, wiring adaptation, software calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Full UK infrastructure compatibility; recommended for regularly used road-legal Hypermini

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Charger with UK Spec
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CHR-ONBD modified for Type 2, JIS adapter included, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Maximum compatibility and reliability; ideal for collector vehicles used on public chargers

problems.recommendation: For museum or static display Hypermini units, an adapter cable is sufficient. For road-legal collector vehicles used regularly, a Type 2-compatible charger retrofit or rebuilt unit offers the best long-term usability and preserves vehicle value.
problems.cta: Enquire about CHR-ONBD charger compatibility for your Nissan Hypermini

problems.group: Don't Let EV Component Failure Write Off Your Nissan Hypermini
problems.h4: Don't Let EV Component Failure Write Off Your Nissan Hypermini
problems.cta: Compare Nissan Hypermini EV Component Prices Now →

years.description: The Hypermini launched with EM57-type motor and LA-XX lead-acid battery for Demo Spec, establishing the pioneering electric microcar platform.
keyChanges: First production Nissan Hypermini units delivered to Japanese fleet customers
EM57-type AC synchronous motor and LA-XX lead-acid battery pack standard
JIS charging protocol and physical connector standard for domestic market
Basic BMS software with limited cell balancing capability

LA-XX - 288V lead-acid battery pack, 6kWh capacity (Demo Spec)
popularModels: Nissan Hypermini Museum Display Early Unit
knownFor: Pioneering urban electric microcar with instant torque delivery
Lead-acid battery limits range but reduces initial cost for demonstration
Early BMS software less sophisticated; cell imbalance more common
JIS charging connector requires adapter for modern UK infrastructure

faq.h2: Nissan Hypermini EV Component Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Nissan Hypermini electric motor replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt EM57-type motors for the Nissan Hypermini typically range from £2,800 to £6,500 supply-only in the UK, depending on variant, mileage and component condition. Specialist sourcing is required due to the Hypermini's ultra-rare status (~200 units produced).
keyPoints: EM57-type AC synchronous motor is the sole powerplant for all Hypermini variants
Prices vary by battery type compatibility (LIP-XX lithium-ion vs LA-XX lead-acid)
Specialist EV knowledge required for safe installation and calibration
12-month warranty available on rebuilt units from EngineMarket network specialists
items.cta: Compare rebuilt EM57 motor prices for your Nissan Hypermini

items.question: How do I identify my Nissan Hypermini motor or battery code?
items.answer: Your Nissan Hypermini's component codes are located on the compliance plate near the driver's door sill, on the motor housing, and within the BMS software readout via specialist diagnostic equipment.
keyPoints: Compliance plate shows variant (Fleet/Demo/Export) and production year
Motor housing stamp indicates EM57-type designation and serial number
BMS diagnostic readout confirms battery code (LIP-XX or LA-XX) and software version
DVLA registration lookup via EngineMarket instantly verifies variant and codes
items.cta: Get your Nissan Hypermini component code verified free

items.question: Will a Nissan Hypermini component from a different variant fit my vehicle?
items.answer: Component compatibility depends on battery type (LIP-XX vs LA-XX), BMS software version, and charging protocol (JIS vs Type 2); cross-variant fitting requires specialist verification to avoid costly errors.
keyPoints: EM57-type motor is physically compatible across all variants
Battery packs require matching BMS software and connector configuration
Export Spec Type 2 chargers need protocol adaptation for early Fleet/Demo units
EngineMarket's variant compatibility checker prevents misselection before ordering
items.cta: Check Nissan Hypermini component compatibility for your variant

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt and used Nissan Hypermini components?
items.answer: Rebuilt components undergo full disassembly, wear-part replacement and dyno validation with warranty; used parts are donor-vehicle sourced with basic testing, suitable for display or temporary use.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units include upgraded bearings, seals and electronics with 12-month warranty
Used components are cost-effective for museum displays or non-critical applications
Rebuilt parts offer provenance documentation valuable for collector vehicle preservation
EngineMarket specialists can advise on the best option for your Hypermini's intended use
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Nissan Hypermini component options
